meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Inertia
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template to overcome unfinished projects. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity template solves project inertia by decoupling architectural scaffolding from business logic. By automating the boilerplate and enforcing a modular directory structure, you bypass the 'blank page' syndrome. This allows you to focus purely on high-impact features, ensuring you reach a deployable MVP within your first weekend.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished side projects is paved with good intentions and broken architecture. You start with high energy, only to watch your motivation evaporate under the crushing weight of configuration, environment variables, and folder structures that lead nowhere. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you are failing because you lack a system that bridges the gap between 'Hello World' and a production-ready application.

Imagine a workflow where the heavy lifting is handled by an automated architect. The Antigravity project template turns your development environment into a launchpad, not a cage. By integrating with Claude Code, you transform from a struggling student into an efficient builder who ships, iterates, and learns—shifting your focus from debugging boilerplate to solving real-world problems.

steps.title: Initialize the Scaffold
steps.description: Create the foundation. This ensures your project follows industry-standard conventions immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity template with Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase integrations. Set up a modular folder structure.

steps.title: Define Core Domain Logic
steps.description: Establish the 'why' of your app. We define the schema before writing UI code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for an AI-powered note-taking app. Include tables for users, notes, and AI-generated summaries.

steps.title: Implement Feature Slices
steps.description: Avoid monolithic files. Build features in isolation to keep your codebase maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a feature slice for 'AI Summarization' that connects the Supabase trigger to an OpenAI API endpoint.

steps.title: Automate UI Components
steps.description: Use the template's component library to build your interface without custom CSS struggles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a dashboard layout using the Antigravity component library that includes a sidebar, header, and a card-based note viewer.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Ship your project to a live URL. Completion is the best teacher.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for Vercel and verify all environment variables are mapped correctly.

pro_tips: Always commit your progress after every successful feature slice to maintain a 'green' streak.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to keep track of your project's architectural decisions across sessions.
Limit your project scope to one core problem; the Antigravity template is designed for deep, focused functionality.
Rename your components immediately to reflect business logic, not just their generic type.
Review the generated code in the 'lib' folder to understand how the template handles authentication middleware.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, share it in the community discord, collect feedback, and refactor one component to improve performance based on user reports.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a folder of unfinished code. Build, ship, and repeat until you become the developer you aspire to be. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
